prompt_text: Best product analytics alternatives for mid-market to enterprise product teams teams on a budget
response:
For **mid-market to enterprise product teams on a budget**, the strongest alternatives are usually **PostHog**, **OpenPanel**, **GA4**, and sometimes **Amplitude** if you can fit the free or lower-cost tiers; **Heap** and **Pendo** are generally better suited when budget is less constrained.[1][2]

- **PostHog** — Best if you want an all-in-one platform with product analytics, session replay, and feature flags, and you can trade some implementation effort for lower cost.[1]
- **OpenPanel** — Best balance of features, affordability, and privacy for most teams looking for a cheaper product analytics stack.[1]
- **GA4** — Best on pure price, especially if you already use Google tooling; it is free up to 25 million events per month, but it is strongest for teams that also need web analytics.[1]
- **Amplitude** — Good for high-volume products that can use the generous free tier, but it is still positioned more toward mid-market and enterprise budgets than startup-style pricing.[1][2]
- **Heap** — Useful for enterprise teams that value retroactive analysis, but the pricing is typically premium and less budget-friendly.[1][2]
- **Pendo** — Best when you need analytics plus in-app guidance and onboarding, but it is not the cheapest option.[1][6]

If your priority is **lowest cost with real product-analytics capability**, the best short list is **PostHog**, **OpenPanel**, and **GA4**.[1] If you need **enterprise-friendly workflows** and can spend more, **Amplitude** and **Heap** are the more common next-step options.[1][2]

A practical way to choose:
- Choose **PostHog** if you want the broadest feature set at a relatively lower price.[1]
- Choose **OpenPanel** if privacy and affordability matter most.[1]
- Choose **GA4** if budget is the main constraint and your use case is mostly event and web analytics.[1]
- Choose **Amplitude** if you expect high event volume and want a mature enterprise product analytics platform.[1][2]
- Choose **Heap** if retroactive analysis is a must-have and budget is secondary.[1][2]
- Choose **Pendo** if product analytics needs to be paired with onboarding and in-app messaging.[1][6]
